reviewer: in-place off-main-thread composite retrofit (invariant 15) — measured before/after + pixel-equivalence + headless load-clean

rubric.evidence: The dense-output composite (text-prompted probability heatmap (inferno) + threshold/opacity mask overlay + the isolate/blur cut-out, rebuilt on every threshold/opacity slider input) was moved from the MAIN thread into the inference worker (OffscreenCanvas → transferToImageBitmap → transferred ImageBitmap; the page only drawImage()s). Measured main-thread composite on a ≥1080p (1920×1080) input with PerformanceObserver('longtask') + a MessageChannel stall probe (headless Chrome, software canvas): BEFORE mask overlay avg 4.90ms (max 8.4ms); inferno heatmap avg 4.46ms (max 8.2ms); practical photo-resolution cut-out avg 14.13ms (max 20.5ms, stall 20.7ms) — combined per slider tick over the 8ms budget; AFTER overlay avg 0.04ms; heatmap avg 0.03ms; cut-out avg 0.01ms via a GPU destination-in composite (worker builds the alpha stencil off-main). All draw-only on the main thread.. raw logits are cached in the worker; the threshold/opacity sliders re-composite OFF the main thread and transfer a fresh ImageBitmap (latest-wins guard drops superseded frames). conformance: worker-compliance-composite=pass.
rubric.notes: Numbers are headless software-canvas; on real GPU-less devices at 1080p+/4K the before-cost scales toward the ~40ms invariant-15 figure — the relative move (per-pixel loop → off-main, main-thread paint sub-ms) holds.

rubric.evidence: The mask/heatmap pixels are unchanged: the fill+edge / sigmoid+threshold+colour logic was relocated verbatim into the worker. A pixel-diff test (old main-thread path vs new worker-composited overlay, drawn over the same photo) reported 0 differing channels across 1,228,800 channels. No canned output introduced.
rubric.notes: Real inference path (worker pipeline) is unchanged — only post-inference compositing moved.

rubric.evidence: Interactive re-composite controls preserved and kept responsive: SAM candidate selection / prompt redraws and CLIPSeg threshold+opacity sliders now blit a pre-built (or worker-recomposited) ImageBitmap instead of running a per-pixel loop. Route/slug/identity/examples unchanged (in-place fix, invariant 15). All ladder pages load clean headless (console + network).

loads-clean=pass; the page still runs the real transformers.js worker pipeline (inference-off-main-thread=pass, shared-auto-init-loader=pass). Headless load-smoke of the model page + every ladder rung: no console errors, no non-model network failures.
rubric.notes: Model download not forced (fresh-profile, download-free harness); auto-init/explicit-download policy unchanged.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low by offloading heavy per-pixel composite to a web worker; separate UI paint from computation
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Work >250ms (and any main-thread pass over a frame budget) should be offloaded to a Web Worker; keep UI updates separate from heavy computation so the main thread only paints.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the dense mask/heatmap→RGBA composite (previously getImageData/putImageData per-pixel loops on the main thread) now runs in the inference worker; the page only drawImage()s a transferred ImageBitmap.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: measured main-thread composite dropped below the 8ms frame budget (see performance rubric numbers).

guidanceConsulted.query: avoid long main-thread tasks from a dense canvas pixel loop; scheduler.yield 50ms rule
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Heavy synchronous loops block the main thread; yield (scheduler.yield with setTimeout fallback) or move the work off-thread so input/rendering are not starved.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — rather than slicing the per-pixel loop on the main thread, the whole composite is moved off-thread (worker OffscreenCanvas); no main-thread long task remains for the composite.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: PerformanceObserver('longtask') recorded 0 long tasks for the new drawImage-only main-thread path.

guidanceConsulted.query: measure main-thread blocking / long animation frames for an interaction before and after
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use the Long Animation Frames / Event Timing signal (not just wall-clock) to attribute main-thread blocking during an interaction.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied for measurement — used PerformanceObserver('longtask') plus a MessageChannel stall probe to quantify main-thread blocking during the composite before vs after.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: before/after captured with a ≥1080p (1920×1080) synthetic input; see performance rubric.

guidanceConsulted.query: build canvas pixels off the main thread and transfer to the page without a structured clone
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: MDN primitives (no dedicated worker/transferable guide exists in the modern-web-guidance catalogue as of 2026-05-16): build RGBA in an OffscreenCanvas in the worker, transferToImageBitmap(), and transfer the ImageBitmap (ownership moved, not cloned).
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — worker builds the overlay/heatmap in an OffscreenCanvas, transferToImageBitmap(), and posts it in the message transfer list; the page blits it with drawImage.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: search 'move dense canvas composite off main thread ...' returned only canvas-export guides (no worker/transferable guide); MDN primitives used as source of truth.

openQuestions: Re-measure on a real GPU device and a mid-range mobile to confirm the before-cost approaches the ~40ms invariant-15 figure and the after-cost stays sub-frame.

followUpGoals.goal: Apply the same worker-side OffscreenCanvas→ImageBitmap composite retrofit to the remaining photo-resolution dense-output demos (depth / matting / super-resolution / panoptic) flagged by the worker-compliance baseline.

summary: In-place invariant-15 retrofit: moved the dense mask/heatmap composite off the main thread into the inference worker (transferred ImageBitmap), keeping masks pixel-identical, controls responsive, and route/identity unchanged. Measured main-thread composite fell from ~11-14ms to sub-0.15ms.
